items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed indefinitely
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street was cancelled on April 8, 2026 with no new hearing date set. Residents who were expecting to weigh in on this project will need to wait for future notice.

items.headline: Major AI Company Expands San José Footprint with $4.1M Office
items.summary: Figure AI is undertaking a significant second-floor office tenant improvement at 4000 N 1st St, signaling continued tech industry investment in San José. This $4.1M expansion reflects ongoing demand for office space among AI and advanced manufacturing companies.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project Moves Forward
items.summary: City Council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project on April 7th. This infrastructure project will improve traffic and safety at the intersection of vehicle and rail traffic on Bascom Avenue.

items.headline: City updates Active Transportation Plan with revised criteria
items.summary: Cupertino is advancing Phase 2 of its Active Transportation Plan, which shapes how the city prioritizes biking, walking, and transit projects. The council reviewed plan edits and new scoring criteria that will determine which projects get built next.

items.headline: City Council Approves Construction Contract for Infrastructure Project
items.summary: The council approved project plans, specifications, and awarded a construction contract, though the specific project details are not fully described in the agenda. Residents may want to follow up to learn what infrastructure improvements are underway in their neighborhoods.

items.headline: Milpitas Updates Federal Housing and Community Development Plan
items.summary: The council considered a substantial amendment to the 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan and 2025-2026 budget, which guides how federal housing and community development funds are spent in Milpitas. This could affect affordable housing initiatives and neighborhood improvements.

items.headline: New 6-Plex Approved for Seely Avenue Development
items.summary: Trimble Summerhill is building a 6-unit multi-family complex at 0 Seely Av ($2.07M), adding meaningful housing supply in an area seeing residential growth. This represents substantial new rental or ownership housing rather than single-family development.

items.headline: City Council Approves Emergency Interim Housing Siting Policy
items.summary: San José adopted a new emergency interim housing siting policy on April 1st, which will affect where the city can place temporary housing solutions for unhoused residents. This policy change could influence future homeless services locations across the city.
